# Product Overview

Status: live
Audience: PM, leadership, design, engineering, go-to-market
Owner: ProductOS PM
Updated At: 2026-04-27
Source Artifacts: `item_lifecycle_state_pm_lifecycle_visibility`, `lifecycle_stage_snapshot_full_lifecycle_pm_visibility`, `runtime_scenario_report_ws_productos_v2_v7_lifecycle_traceability`, `validation_lane_report_ws_productos_v2_v7_lifecycle_traceability`

## What ProductOS Is

ProductOS is a product operating system for product managers.

It combines structured product state, readable decision documents, governed communication, and bounded AI-agent workflows so PMs can move from noisy inputs to aligned execution with less manual reconstruction.

## Who It Is For

ProductOS is for PMs who need help with:

- turning fragmented signals into decision-ready problem framing
- keeping strategy, roadmap, and PRD work consistent
- creating stakeholder-safe docs and communication quickly
- closing feedback loops into bounded improvements instead of recurring churn

## Problem It Solves

Most PM work breaks when the same truth has to be reconstructed repeatedly across notes, JSON, decks, status updates, and planning discussions.

ProductOS reduces that reconstruction burden by keeping structured state and readable outputs linked through one governed operating model.

## How It Works

ProductOS runs through a small set of golden loops:

- signal to product decision
- decision to stakeholder alignment
- feedback to accepted improvement

Each loop uses artifacts, readable docs, validation lanes, and explicit PM approval instead of one opaque AI-agent route.

## Why It Is Different

ProductOS is not only a note-taking tool, a planning board, or a slide generator.

It is differentiated by:

- structured product artifacts that remain machine-readable
- readable docs that stay linked to source truth
- presentation and communication outputs that are governed rather than improvised
- Ralph-loop quality control for bounded high-stakes slices
- reference evidence that the system is used to improve itself

## Current Release Reality

The current public stable line is ProductOS `V8.4.0`.

The current bounded external claim is:

- ProductOS provides a governed PM operating model with mission-to-strategy discovery, reusable research packets, prioritization context, execution artifacts, and bounded visual outputs
- the stable line keeps PM review, proof posture, and release boundaries explicit across those surfaces
- visual outputs remain part of the governed operating model, not a separate promise that outranks lifecycle coherence

The current non-goals remain explicit:

- broad autonomous PM replacement claims
- open-ended swarm execution claims
- generalized market intelligence for every product question
- broad external publishing and distribution automation beyond the current evidence-backed surfaces

The active internal follow-on program is lifecycle enrichment reconciliation:

- `P0` runtime and governed-doc coherence
- `P1` governed research and prioritization enrichment
- `P2` downstream learning, traceability, and reopen behavior

That program should strengthen the repo and starter/adoption surfaces without broadening the current public claim set.
